title: TMC submits response to EC, rejects rebel faction's claim as 'fraudulent'
content: TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee said the party had filed a "very detailed reply" to the Election Commission in response to a representation made by rebel leader Ritabrata Banerjee

title: ECI asks both factions of TMC to submit responses on organisational elections
content: Rebel faction meets a full Bench of the EC; declining to disclose the documents submitted to the poll body, Ritabrata Banerjee says they ‘abided by all rules’ in conducting an organisational session on June 22

title: Mamata hits out at Trinamool rebels, dares them to join BJP
content: The former West Bengal Chief Minister, broke her weeks-long silence when her party had split with a majority of MLAs and MPs deciding to switch their loyalties after the defeat in the Assembly polls; accuses the rebels of betraying the party after winning on the party symbol

title: Amit Shah announces new cooperative life insurance company on lines of Bharat Taxi
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah said the establishment of the Ministry has given a new ‘lifeline’ to India's cooperative movement

title: PM Modi arrives in Indonesia to further strengthen India's Act East Policy
content: PM Modi said he will hold discussions with President Subianto “aimed at adding even more momentum to this partnership across diverse sectors”

title: New road to integrated bus stand to be completed in 18 months, says Tiruchi Collector
content: Once completed, the 10.11-km-long bypass built by Tiruchi Corporation, is expected to drastically reduce the travel time between two national highways on the outskirts of Tiruchi

title: Ram temple donation row exposed power struggle within BJP: Akhilesh Yadav
content: Akhilesh Yadav stated that the BJP leadership is more concerned about internal rivalries than protecting the religious faith of the people

title: Ram temple donation row: VHP urges SIT to question Opposition leaders over theft claims
content: The letter specifically names SP leader Prof. Ram Gopal Yadav, AAP national convenor Arvind Kejriwal, AAP Rajya Sabha MP Sanjay Singh, and Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi Vadra

title: Software engineer killed after car crashes into lorry on ORR near Shamshabad
content: The police said the car allegedly crashed into the rear of an unidentified lorry that had suddenly slowed down in the same lane. Efforts underway to identify the lorry.

title: Meghalaya CM meets Amit Shah, conveys FCRA amendment concerns
content: Mr. Sangma highlights how the proposed changes could disrupt the functioning of religious, educational, and charitable institutions that have long complemented government efforts

title: 301 ticketless passengers on single train fined in Tamil Nadu
content: ₹2.64 lakh was collected as penalty by the Salem Railway Division during a special intensive ticket-checking drive carried out on the Coimbatore-Silchar Weekly Express (Train No. 15627)

title: 450 private buses stay off roads in Kasaragod as operators protest free travel scheme for women
content: The strike was called by the Kasaragod District Private Bus Operators Federation, along with various workers’ organisations

title: Telangana gears up for second edition of global investors summit in December 2026
content: CM directs officials to further develop Taramati Baradari and Durgam Cheruvu converted into a full scale tourist attraction

title: Legacy of dialogue process pursued by Vajpayee, Manmohan Singh needs to be revived: Mirwaiz
content: Says meaningful dialogue is imperative between Delhi and Islamabad, and equally between Delhi and Srinagar for peace

title: Markets surge to fourth day, led by bank stocks, lower oil prices
content: In four trading days, the BSE benchmark has jumped 2.36%, and the Nifty surged 564.6 points, or 2.36%

title: Iran’s move to invite Sunni Kashmir leader Mehbooba conveys a message
content: Mehbooba Mufti attending supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s final rites is bound to change the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) president’s status in politics now onwards

title: Lionel Messi’s 39th birthday celebrations in Bengal’s Ichhapur
content: A priest, a life sized statue, a 39-pound cake and a tea seller’s lifelong devotion. Inside Lionel Messi’s annual birthday celebrations in West Bengal’s Ichhapur

title: Modi government has put India's education system into ICU: Congress
content: Congress accuses the Modi government of threatening India’s education system; demands Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation

title: Three-year-old swept away by stream found dead near Taliparamba
content: The accident occurred near the Kodleri bridge at Poomangalam when the child had accompanied his relatives to the stream for washing clothes and bathing

title: First-time BJP MLA Habbey Teron unanimously elected deputy Speaker of Assam Assembly
content: Mr. Teron is an MLA from the newly formed Amri constituency, which came into existence after the delimitation exercise of 2023

title: YouTuber Prasna Ravan remanded in custody as Krishna police invoke UAPA
content: The Gannavaram police invoked the Act over a complaint alleging he propagated Maoist ideology at an Eluru meeting; rights groups criticised the move, and the JSP sought action against two YouTubers

title: KDA seeks Kannada language teaching in all government, private and technical institutions
content: KDA Chairperson Purushotham Bilimale objects to notification issued by the Technical Education Department for the appointment of teaching posts not accommodating the appointment of Kannada language instructors

title: Manipur CM hands over ₹1 lakh each to 26 chronically ill IDPs
content: The beneficiaries have been living at the Lamboikhongnangkhong relief camp in State capital Imphal since the ethnic conflict between the Kuki-Zo and Meitei communities erupted in May 2023. They are suffering from kidney ailments, cancer, and other diseases.

title: Union demands removal of 12-hour duty roster after railway pointsman dies in Nagpur
content: "The catastrophic consequences of severe mental and physical fatigue were painfully demonstrated in Nagpur Railway," said Central Organising Secretary of the All India Pointsmen Association (AIPMA)

title: G.D.N. film trailer launched amid vintage cars in Coimbatore’s motorsports circuit
content: Coimbatore’s Kari Motor Speedway played host to the launch of the trailer of G.D.N., a biopic on engineering pioneer GD Naidu, played by actor Madhavan

title: Food facilities sought for CAR personnel posted on duty across Bengaluru
content: CAR personnel spend between ₹80 and ₹120 a day on meals and are frequently compelled to eat roadside food, resulting in additional financial burden and health concerns

title: Bikers perform wheelies in front of ambulance, endanger patient’s life in Bengaluru
content: According to witnesses, the riders were not wearing helmets nor were number plates visible in the two-wheelers

title: Environmental collective writes to Congress leadership against ‘anti-environmental actions’ by party MPs, MLAs in Kerala
content: Coexistence Collective, a broad coalition of environmental organisations, wildlife conservationists, researchers, scientists and civil society groups, alleges that some Congress leaders are attempting to undermine forest, wildlife and environmental protection, and such actions have intensified since UDF came into power in Kerala

title: Potholes, now landslide: Rs 6,695-cr Mumbai-Pune expressway link could not survive one monsoon
content: Heavy rains triggered a landslide near the Missing Link tunnel, forcing the closure of the Mumbai-Pune Expressway and old highway on Monday. This disruption, barely nine weeks after its inauguration, also impacted rail traffic, leading to train cancellations and diversions. The incident follows earlier concerns about the new stretch's road quality, highlighting ongoing challenges with the ambitious infrastructure project.

title: Watch: PM Modi's plane escorted by fighter jets, welcomed by Indonesia's president
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi commenced a three-nation tour with a grand welcome in Indonesia, highlighting the strong Comprehensive Strategic Partnership. Discussions are set to focus on defense, maritime cooperation, and critical minerals, with Indonesia being a key partner for India's supply chain and energy transition. The visit aims to deepen institutional cooperation and strengthen trade ties, building on existing momentum in the Indo-Pacific region.

title: Twist in Balogun row: Belgium allowed to appeal against FIFA decision; what it means
content: Balogun had been due to miss the United States' FIFA World Cup round-of-16 match against Belgium after receiving a direct red card against Bosnia and Herzegovina, a dismissal that normally carries an automatic one-game suspension. However, FIFA announced on Sunday that "the one-game ban had been suspended," making the striker available for selection.

title: Weeks before Alexander Wang’s AI update tease, Google ‘messaged’ Meta
content: Google capped Meta's use of Gemini AI models around March 2026 after Meta sought more computing capacity than Google could provide. The restriction disrupted Meta's internal AI projects and forced employees to ration AI tokens. It came weeks before Meta AI chief Alexandr Wang announced a Muse Spark update aimed at cutting the company's dependence on rival models for coding and agentic tasks.

title: Unlike Ponting or Kallis: Why Root has a genuine shot at Tendulkar's 15,921
content: Joe Root now stands second on the all-time Test run charts with 14,114 runs, leaving him 1,807 short of Sachin Tendulkar's record. Plenty of batters have climbed into the conversation over the last decade before fading away. The difference this time isn't just the gap. It's where Root is in his career.

title: Red, blue, saffron: Buses in Bengal show changing colours of politics
content: Kolkata's government buses are undergoing a significant colour change, shifting from blue-and-white to saffron, a move the transport minister attributes to modernization and a "dynamic double-engine regime." This rebranding extends to e-tickets, with officials maintaining it's not a formal policy shift. However, critics argue cosmetic changes distract from critical issues like driver shortages and declining fleet strength, with commuters prioritizing reliability over political symbolism.

title: China's Agibot completes world's first livestream of humanoid robots
content: Chinese firm Agibot has showcased the world's first live broadcast of humanoid robots on a tablet production line. Eight Agibot G2 robots worked non-stop for six days at Longcheer Technology, successfully inspecting and sorting 17,625 tablets with a near-perfect success rate. This demonstration highlights the practical application of embodied AI in real-world industrial settings, working alongside human employees and existing machinery.

title: 'Crossed red line': UEFA blasts FIFA over Balogun suspension after Trump intervention
content: UEFA has slammed FIFA's decision to allow US forward Folarin Balogun to play against Belgium despite a red card, calling it "incomprehensible" and a "red line" crossed. Balogun's one-game ban was surprisingly suspended by FIFA, a move that has drawn outrage from Belgium and criticism from UEFA for undermining the "integrity" of the competition.

title: Archaeologists find another cannonball from the 1836 Battle of the Alamo
content: Archaeologists have unearthed a cannonball near the Alamo Church, potentially fired during the 1836 battle. This discovery follows a similar find earlier this year, with both projectiles believed to have remained undisturbed for nearly two centuries. These solid iron and bronze shots offer tangible evidence of the intense artillery exchange during the pivotal siege, adding significant historical weight to ongoing restoration efforts at the Alamo.

title: China is building a different kind of AI beyond classic chatbots
content: China is pioneering a distinct AI approach, prioritizing systems for real-time urban, industrial, and logistical coordination over Western chatbot and AGI focus. This strategy emphasizes prediction, management, and continuous adaptation within complex environments.  By viewing AI as essential infrastructure, China aims to create intelligent systems that govern movement and respond dynamically to change, reflecting a broader, process-oriented understanding of intelligence.

title: Jamie Dimon gives a 'reality check' to Canada's PM Mark Carney on his unity call to Europe
content: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has dismissed Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney's call for middle powers to unite, calling it a "fantasy." Dimon pointed to Europe's economic struggles as an example of cooperation's limitations, citing high taxes and regulation. He emphasized that attractive business environments are crucial for growth, contrasting Europe's declining competitiveness with the U.S. market. Dimon also reassured investors about America's investment appeal, noting China's evolving global approach.

title: 'Why Diljit Dosanjh chooses controversial films': FWICE President BN Tiwari amid 'Satluj' row
content: Following the abrupt and highly controversial removal of Honey Trehan’s biographical drama 'Satluj' (originally titled 'Punjab ’95') from streaming platform ZEE5 India just 48 hours after its premiere, Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) President BN Tiwari has strongly criticized lead actor Diljit Dosanjh. Speaking exclusively to IANS, Tiwari expressed deep surprise over the Punjabi superstar's recurring inclination toward politically sensitive and controversial scripts, emphasizing that global icons must keep "nation first" and avoid choosing roles purely for financial gain.

title: 18 killed as Russia fires 68 missiles, 351 drones at Ukraine
content: Russia unleashed a massive missile and drone assault on Ukraine, claiming at least 18 lives and injuring scores, with Kyiv bearing the brunt. Ukrainian officials highlighted critical air defence gaps, particularly against ballistic missiles, due to a shortage of Patriot interceptor systems. President Zelenskyy renewed his urgent plea to allies for more interceptors, emphasizing the vulnerability to continued Russian attacks on civilian areas.

title: Watch: Paraglider crash-lands on busy Chandigarh-Manali highway after wind shift
content: A paraglider suffered injuries after an emergency landing on the Chandigarh-Manali National Highway near Manali on Sunday. Strong winds diverted the pilot from his intended landing site, leading to a crash. He was taken to Kullu hospital and is reported to be stable. This incident follows a similar event last month where a tandem paraglider also faced strong winds but landed safely.

title: Jude Bellingham's net worth 2026: How rich is he with Ashlyn Castro?
content: Jude Bellingham, the English midfielder, has been a force to be reckoned with on the field this year as millions cheer him on. Through the last four matches, Jude Bellingham recorded 2 goals and 1 assist and is easily one of the most popular and reliable players playing in the 2026 World Cup. Away from the field, Jude Bellingham has built a beautiful life with his girlfriend, popular social media influencer Ashlyn Castro.

title: Why Harrison Butker wasn't invited to Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift's wedding ?
content: Controversial comments by Kansas City Chiefs placekicker Harrison Butker regarding women’s roles and other social issues had sparked widespread debate in 2024. While teammates like Patrick Mahomes expressed disagreement but acknowledged Butker as a person, Travis Kelce defended him as a good teammate, though not endorsing his views back then. Amidst this, a viral rumor suggests Butker was reportedly uninvited to Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift’s royal wedding. Despite most of the team receiving invitations from their star tight end.

title: 'Fresh Paneer' to 'vegan': FSSAI issues notices to multiple brands over misleading claims
content: India's food regulator, FSSAI, has issued notices to several brands, including 'Heritage Fresh Paneer', for misleading claims on their packaging. The authority is cracking down on labels that could deceive consumers, such as 'fresh' paneer or 'vegan' spreads. This move aims to ensure food products adhere to strict labelling regulations and prevent any implication of unverified approval.

title: BMW accident claim: Punjab consumer panel orders Bajaj Allianz to pay Rs 20 lakh
content: A car owner's Rs 20 lakh claim for a totaled BMW has been upheld by the Punjab State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission. Bajaj Allianz General Insurance's appeal was dismissed, with the commission ruling their forensic report unreliable. The insurer had denied the claim, stating the damage didn't match the accident account, but the commission found the owner's version credible, citing evidence from the insurer's own investigator.

title: 'India's reputation suffers': Cong hits out at PM Modi as passport ranking slips to 125th
content: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge has sharply criticized the Modi government, citing India's declining passport rankings and increased passport fees. He questioned the government's claims of enhanced global respect, pointing to a drop in rankings and stagnant foreign tourist arrivals. Kharge alleged that citizens are paying more for subpar services, impacting India's international standing.

title: Wedding, car, cash: Ram temple donation 'theft' accused reveals Rs 19L spending trail
content: Fresh financial leads have emerged in the Ram Temple donation embezzlement case. Accused Avinash Shukla, during interrogation, reportedly detailed spending of Rs 19 lakh on family and friends, including wedding expenses for brothers and personal purchases like a car. Investigators are examining land deals and bank accounts, with legal action underway to recover funds. Valuables worth lakhs were previously recovered.

title: Mighty Haaland slays Brazil as England survive Mexican shootout
content: On Day 25, England rediscovered its stiff upperlip to beat Mexico with a performance that could have been a Kipling poem. The mighty Viking Erling Haaland slayed Brazil’s Samba boys. And FIFA’s decision to rescind Balogun’s red-card has raised political heckles. Read this and more in today’s Offside.

title: Real estate reset: Inside Musk's 'own no house' pledge & where he lives now
content: Elon Musk's declaration of living without property for liberation has gained attention again. Following the sale of seven homes in California, netting over $100 million, Musk stated he resides in a modular dwelling close to SpaceX. Nevertheless, an investigation uncovered that affiliated companies purchased multiple luxurious homes in Texas, allegedly for the mothers of his kids and their families, which contradicts his commitment to 'own no house.'

title: China’s JL missile family: The submarine-launched nukes designed to reach US
content: China has successfully test-fired a ballistic missile from a nuclear submarine in the western Pacific, highlighting its growing sea-based nuclear deterrent. This development underscores the evolution of Beijing's 'Giant Wave' missile program, from the limited-range JL-1 to the intercontinental JL-3. The new missile significantly enhances China's second-strike capability, allowing submarines to strike distant targets from safer waters.

title: NIA charges Hafiz Saeed as accused in Pahalgam terror attack
content: Pakistan-based terrorist Hafiz Saeed has been formally accused by the NIA in the Pahalgam terror attack. The agency filed a supplementary chargesheet, naming Saeed as the orchestrator of the cross-border conspiracy. He faces charges for waging war against India and his leadership roles in Lashkar-e-Taiba and The Resistance Front. This development follows the NIA's extensive investigation into the April 22 attack.

title: Indian Navy set to commission 6th indigenous Project 17A stealth frigate Mahendragiri on July 11
content: The commissioning of the indigenous stealth frigate Mahendragiri (F38) in Visakhapatnam marks a significant enhancement to India's naval capabilities. Engineered entirely in India, the frigate integrates state-of-the-art indigenous weaponry and sensors, such as BrahMos and Barak-8 missiles. With over 75% of its components sourced locally, Mahendragiri stands as a pillar of India's commitment to self-reliance in defense, poised to protect maritime interests and bolster regional security.

title: Watch: Bellingham's heartwarming gesture to Mora goes viral after WC thriller
content: Jude Bellingham's stellar performance, including a brace, powered England to a thrilling 3-2 World Cup victory over Mexico.  His heartwarming post-match jersey exchange with young Mexican talent Gilberto Mora captivated fans, showcasing mutual respect.  Despite playing with ten men, England secured a quarter-final spot against Norway, with Bellingham also setting a new record for youngest player with 10 World Cup appearances.

title: New Army chief’s 'VIJAY' mantra: How India’s land force is preparing for future wars
content: Army Chief General Dhiraj Seth has unveiled his "VIJAY" mantra, emphasizing Vigilance, Innovation, Jointness, Atmanirbharta, and Yodha First. This strategic direction aligns with the army's Decade of Transformation and Prime Minister Modi's "JAI" directive for integrated defence forces. The initiative aims to bolster capabilities through modernization and doctrinal shifts, preparing the army for future challenges in a rapidly evolving global security landscape.

title: 'Our objective was never to ... ': Prashant Kishor on why he chose to contest Bankipur bypoll
content: Jan Suraaj founder Prashant Kishor is contesting the Bankipur Assembly by-election from the BJP's stronghold to urge voters to transcend caste and religious affiliations. He criticizes the incumbent BJP MLA for abandoning the seat after securing a Rajya Sabha berth, citing Amit Shah as a counter-example. Kishor aims to challenge the BJP's perceived arrogance and their underestimation of Bankipur's educated electorate.

title: Quote of the day by Heath Ledger
content: Heath Ledger once said, "If you make decisions based upon people’s reactions or judgments, then you make really boring choices." This insightful quote underscores the importance of embracing individuality over conforming to societal expectations. The article showcases how Ledger exemplified this belief through daring roles, such as the Joker and a gay cowboy, rejecting norms to authentically express his artistry, ultimately inspiring others to pursue their unique paths.

title: Earth could have over 20 million insect species, leaving most still unknown to science
content: A groundbreaking study suggests Earth may host up to 20.3 million insect species, significantly more than the previously estimated 6 million. Researchers conducted extensive surveys in Costa Rica, scaling findings to a global level. This revelation highlights the vast unknown biodiversity and underscores the urgent need for insect conservation amidst alarming population declines due to human activities.

title: 'Will never forget this gesture of friendship': Iran thanks India for attending Khamenei's funeral
content: Iran's Embassy in India expressed deep gratitude to New Delhi for its participation in the funeral of former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei. The presence of an official delegation, political leaders, and citizens was hailed as a reflection of strong historical, cultural, and human bonds. This gesture of solidarity during Iran's national mourning has been deeply appreciated and will not be forgotten by the Iranian people.

title: More than a flypast: How nations use the sky to send strategic messages
content: Military flypasts, from early ceremonial displays to modern power projections, showcase national strength and technological prowess. Historical events like the post-WWII Tokyo Bay flyover and Cold War Soviet parades highlight their strategic importance. Today, nations like the US, Russia, China, and India use these aerial spectacles to demonstrate military modernization and global ambitions, reflecting shifting geopolitical landscapes.

title: 'A good starting point': Tuchel jokes Kane could ask Trump over Quansah's red card
content: England coach Thomas Tuchel expressed bewilderment after FIFA overturned Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension, questioning the precedent it sets.  He humorously suggested Harry Kane might need to contact Donald Trump to reverse Jarell Quansah's red card, highlighting concerns about consistency in refereeing decisions. Tuchel emphasised the need for clarity on how such rulings are revisited, fearing it could lead to endless appeals.

title: Crude comeback: IOCL, HPCL buy 7 million barrels of energy supplies
content: Indian refiners Indian Oil Corp and Hindustan Petroleum Corp have secured millions of crude barrels from Angola, Nigeria, and Brazil to bolster national reserves.  This strategic procurement follows a significant drawdown in stockpiles due to Strait of Hormuz disruptions.  Inventories have since rebounded, nearing a year-high, signaling a stabilization of India's energy security amidst global market fluctuations and OPEC+ output adjustments.

title: Vinesh Phogat Asian Games trials case: Delhi HC disposes wrestler's plea
content: The Delhi high court has concluded wrestler Vinesh Phogat's plea challenging her initial exclusion from Asian Games trials, deeming it moot as she participated following court orders. However, the Wrestling Federation of India must now decide on a pending show-cause notice within two weeks, ensuring Phogat receives a personal hearing. This decision addresses immediate participation concerns while disciplinary proceedings continue.

title: Anvay Dravid hits blazing 67-ball 87 for India U-19 vs Sri Lanka in second Youth ODI
content: Rahul Dravid's younger son, Anvay, starred with a brilliant 87, anchoring India U-19's 285 against Sri Lanka A. He, along with Arjun Rajput's 76, forged a crucial partnership after an early collapse. This performance follows Anvay's recent success leading Karnataka's U-19s and his elder brother Samit's all-round display in a T20 match, highlighting the Dravid family's cricketing prowess.

title: 25 killed, hundreds wounded after clashes break out in Sri Lankan prison
content: Tragic violence at Sri Lanka's Negombo prison has claimed 19 lives and injured 72, including security personnel. Clashes between rival inmate groups, reportedly over drug trafficking, escalated after prisoners seized firearms. Authorities have deployed special forces to restore order and launched multiple investigations into the deadly unrest. Security remains on high alert.

title: Allahabad HC asks Centre, ASI to file counter-affidavit in 'Tejo Mahalaya' dispute
content: The petition says that the Agreshwar Mahadev Nagnatheswar Virajman Tejo Mahalaya temple is present inside the Taj Mahal premises

title: Road to Sundapur in Erode remains a dream as project awaits final clearance
content: Barring a one-kilometre stretch laid by the panchayat, which is now in a damaged condition, the village has never had a tarred road

title: AAP attacks govt. over top health official appointment
content: The Delhi government’s Anti-Corruption Branch arrested Vatsala Agarwal over the alleged procurement scam involving medicines, surgical items and medical equipment

title: NIA names LeT founder Hafiz Saeed as accused in Pahalgam terror attack
content: The charge sheet provides details of Pakistan’s conspiracy, Saeed’s role, and supporting evidence collected by the NIA in the case; LeT and TRF have been charged as legal entities for their “role in planning, facilitating, and executing the Pahalgam terror attack”

title: Goodwill gestures, rallies, tributes mark first anniversary of Pahalgam attack in J&K
content: Kashmir’s chief cleric Mr. Farooq said the people of J&K remember the Pahalgam victims ‘with deep sorrow’. However, he also drew attention to other massacres witnessed in Kashmir

title: Pahalgam, a year on: Resilience falters, tourism struggles
content: After the initial shock and grief of the April 2025 terrorist attack that killed 26 unarmed men on south Kashmir’s Baisaran meadow, Peerzada Ashiq finds that locals grapple with an uncertain future. Tourism is low, livelihoods have been lost, and heightened security measures are in place

title: Ayyankkuzhi residents to revive protest over alleged non-payment of rent dues
content: The families had to vacate their homes in Ayyankuzhi following a fire at the Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited–Kochi Refinery campus in their neighbourhood nearly a year ago

title: Delhi Metro Rail Corporation seeks turnkey role for proposed Kerala High Speed Railway project
content: The proposed high-speed rail corridor, estimated to cost ₹57,000 crore, will connect Thiruvananthapuram and Kannur. According to DMRC, the project will eventually have the capacity to carry 2.28 lakh passengers a day

title: Diamond jewellery, microart cast in shape of 18K gold rocket among payloads to be launched by Vikram-1
content: On July 6, Skyroot Aerospace announced that India’s first privately developed orbital-class rocket will be carrying six payloads

title: Prisons in India continue to be overcrowded by undertrials | Data
content: More than half of the States/UTs had an occupancy rate of over 100% in 2024 despite a modest increase in capacity

title: Vaishnaw flags off Nanded-Mumbai, Tanakpur-Nanded express trains
content: Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw also announced that the Tanakpur-Pilibhit train service had been extended up to Shahjahanpur in U.P.

title: Pot calling the kettle black: Congress slams VHP letter to police on claims made by Opposition leaders on donations
content: Having itself come under the scanner, the VHP has neither the moral authority nor the credibility to point fingers at the Opposition, K.C. Venugopal said

title: Congress calls for time-bound judicial probe into Ram Mandir ‘missing donations’ row
content: U.P. Congress chief Ajay Rai says the probe by a Special Investigation Team into the matter is a farce, and, ‘The BJP-RSS have committed organised plunder at Shri Ram Janmabhoomi’

title: Ex-BJP MP terms Ram temple funds row ‘serious’; insult to Sanatana Dharma: Akhilesh Yadav
content: Vinay Katiyar claims that some members involved in managing the trust are “thieves”; allegations a “question mark” on faith, accountability and trust, the BJP cannot “evade responsibility”, says Congress

title: Beekeeping initiative in Pichavaram mangroves: a sweet livelihood for Irula tribals
content: As many as 250 bee boxes will be installed across the mangrove ecosystem. The initiative that is expected to enhance pollination and support natural mangrove regeneration, is designed to support 100 Irula tribal households.

title: India Helps Unlock Universe's Secrets, From God Particle To Dark Matter
content: Buried deep underground, this extraordinary machine has already transformed humanity's understanding of the universe.

title: Delhi Police Bust Interstate Racket Selling Fake Governmentt IDs, 2 Arrested
content: Police said the accused operated the website by asking customers to first recharge a digital wallet before placing orders.

title: Indian-Origin Man Poses As 'CIA Agent' To Secure Indonesia Defence Deal
content: Srivastava developed ties with Prabowo Subianto, Indonesia's former defence minister and now the country's president, and reportedly nicknamed himself "Mr G".

title: China Submarine Test Fires Nuclear-Capable Missile Into Pacific
content: China's navy fired a submarine-launched missile into the Pacific, days after warning Pacific nations of the planned test launch

title: 4-Year-Old Mauled To Death By Stray Dogs In Punjab's Hoshiarpur
content: The victim, Gungun, was the daughter of Ram Jeevan and Preeti, residents of Dhyanpur village in Uttar Pradesh's Lakhimpur Kheri district.

title: Delhi Police Arrest Man After 29 Years For Murder Of Co-Worker In 1997
content: Police said the accused worked as a Plaster of Paris (POP) artisan during his years in hiding and frequently changed locations to avoid arrest.

title: 'Dowry, Belt Beatings': Delhi Woman's Mother On 72-Day Marriage Before Death
content: The family has alleged that Akriti had been subjected to continuous harassment over dowry since her marriage. They claimed that her husband and his relatives had been demanding Rs 20 lakh and had repeatedly threatened and mistreated her.

title: 4 Killed, 5 Injured After Truck Collides With SUV In Madhya Pradesh's Sehore
content: Four people travelling in the SUV were killed on the spot, while five others sustained injuries and were admitted to a hospital, an official said.

title: Video: F-16 And Sukhoi-30 Welcome PM Modi's Aircraft In Indonesian Airspace
content: PM Narendra Modi in a post on X said he was "touched by President Prabowo Subianto's gesture of welcoming me at the airport."

title: "Wishing A Long, Healthy Life": PM Modi Greets Dalai Lama On Birthday
content: The Dalai Lama was born on July 6, 1935 to a Tibetan farming family in the small village of Taktser, located at Amdo in Tibet.

title: College Girl, 17, Attacked With Knife At Karnataka Bus Stop, Accused Flees
content: The accused has been identified as 28-year-old Javvar, a resident of Montimaru Padpu, who reportedly runs a grocery shop near the bus stop.

title: PM Modi's Indonesia Visit to Unveil Major Defence, Digital Push: Envoy
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's visit to Indonesia is set to mark a significant upgrade in India's strategic partnership with Southeast Asia's largest economy.

title: Head Injury, Sexual Assault: Autopsy Of Bengal Girl Who Was Stuffed In Sack
content: Police arrested the key accused in the case on Monday, taking the total number of arrests in the case to three.

title: Pipeline Work To Hit Water Supply In Delhi: Check List Of Affected Areas
content: As per an advisory issued by the New Delhi Municipal Council (NDMC), water supply will remain affected during the evening of July 6 and morning of July 7.

title: "Like Freedom": How Armenia Became Safe Haven For Russians Fleeing Putin
content: Since 2022, Armenia's capital, Yerevan, has become a safe place for tens of thousands of Russians who left their country to avoid the war in Ukraine.

title: Analysis: Is Bangladesh's China Pivot Just Positioning For Negotiations With India
content: Xi Jinping has takeaways for India as it signals China's permanent presence in Bangladesh, especially in the Teesta region.

title: INS Mahendragiri, India's 6th Stealth Frigate, To Join Navy Fleet On July 11
content: It is the first warship to carry the name Mahendragiri and the sixth ship of the Nilgiri-class under Project 17A.

title: "Could Be Misused By Anti-India Forces": Sources On Why 'Satluj' Was Pulled Down
content: Originally named 'Punjab 95', the movie was released on ZEE5 under the new title Satluj on July 3, after years of delay and controversies.

title: Andhra Fishermen Rescued After Boat Breaks Down Off Odisha Coast
content: Ten fishermen from Chintapalli village in Andhra Pradesh's Vizianagaram district were rescued after their mechanised fishing boat developed an engine failure and drifted off the Odisha coast near Puri, officials said.
